label: Business Continuity / DR Manager
description: Develops and manages business continuity, disaster recovery, and organizational resilience programs — BIA, recovery strategy design, plan development, testing, and maintenance.

paths.name: DRI International Track
paths.rationale: DRI International CBCP is the most recognized BC certification in North America. CBCP to MBCP validates increasing competency in business continuity program management.
pros: CBCP is the most recognized BC credential in North America
MBCP validates advanced competency in complex BC programs
Strong demand in financial services, healthcare, and government
cons: Require professional experience and peer review
DRI primarily North America-recognized — BCI is stronger in Europe

prerequisites.experience: 2 years BC/DR experience required for CBCP.

cert_sequence.notes: DRI Associate Business Continuity Professional — entry-level DRI credential with no experience requirement. Good starting credential while accumulating experience toward CBCP. Study with DRI Professional Practices for Business Continuity Management.

DRI Certified Business Continuity Professional — requires 2 years experience and qualifying exam. Most recognized BC certification in North America. Study with DRI Professional Practices and BCI Good Practice Guidelines.

DRI Master Business Continuity Professional — requires 5 years experience, written examination, and submission of a professional paper demonstrating BC program leadership. The MBCP distinguishes senior BC professionals who have led programs from those who participated in them.

paths.name: ISO 22301 Track
paths.rationale: For BC/DR managers in organizations pursuing formal ISO 22301 BCMS certification — or those working with European and Asia-Pacific clients where ISO standards are preferred.
pros: ISO 22301 LI and LA widely recognized for BCMS implementation and audit work
BCI credentials add professional body recognition
cons: Less recognized than DRI credentials in North American market

prerequisites.experience: BC or risk management experience beneficial.

cert_sequence.notes: BCI Certificate — BCI foundational credential covering Good Practice Guidelines. Strong European recognition with lower experience requirements than DRI CBCP.
ISO 22301 Lead Implementer — BCMS design, implementation, and management per ISO 22301. Requires 5-day accredited training course. Primary credential for leading ISO 22301 implementation projects.
BCI Member — advanced BCI credential requiring 5 years demonstrated BC experience and professional assessment. Senior BCI credential recognized in European and global BC programs.
